exercises.title: Compare Spring AI vs LangChain4j APIs
exercises.instructions:
For each task, show the Spring AI approach and the LangChain4j approach:

A: Simple one-turn Q&A with system message
B: Multi-turn conversation with memory
C: RAG with vector store retrieval
D: Tool calling (AI calls a Java method)
exercises.expected_solution:
A: Simple Q&A:
   Spring AI: ChatClient.Builder → .defaultSystem(msg) → .prompt().user(q).call().content()
   LangChain4j: interface with @SystemMessage → AiServices.create(I.class, model) → i.answer(q)
   Winner: LangChain4j (one interface declaration vs several lines of setup)

B: Multi-turn with memory:
   Spring AI: Manual List<Message> per session → inject into prompt on each call
   LangChain4j: interface method with @MemoryId String sessionId → MessageWindowChatMemory
                 LangChain4j manages history automatically per sessionId
   Winner: LangChain4j (automatic memory management via @MemoryId)

C: RAG:
   Spring AI: VectorStore.similaritySearch() → build context string → include in .user() message
   LangChain4j: ContentRetriever → AiServices.builder().contentRetriever(retriever).build()
                RAG injected automatically — no manual context building
   Winner: LangChain4j (RAG wired declaratively via builder)

D: Tool calling:
   Spring AI: @Tool annotation + ChatClient.Builder.defaultTools(bean) + .prompt().call()
   LangChain4j: @Tool annotation + AiServices.builder().tools(bean).build()
   Winner: Tie — both are similar; Spring AI slightly more Spring-idiomatic

OVERALL:
  LangChain4j: cleaner interfaces, simpler memory/RAG, less boilerplate
  Spring AI: better Spring Boot integration, more control over prompt details
  Production choice: depends on team preference; both are production-ready

test_cases.expected:
A: Simple Q&A:
   Spring AI: ChatClient.Builder → .defaultSystem(msg) → .prompt().user(q).call().content()
   LangChain4j: interface with @SystemMessage → AiServices.create(I.class, model) → i.answer(q)
   Winner: LangChain4j (one interface declaration vs several lines of setup)

B: Multi-turn with memory:
   Spring AI: Manual List<Message> per session → inject into prompt on each call
   LangChain4j: interface method with @MemoryId String sessionId → MessageWindowChatMemory
                 LangChain4j manages history automatically per sessionId
   Winner: LangChain4j (automatic memory management via @MemoryId)

C: RAG:
   Spring AI: VectorStore.similaritySearch() → build context string → include in .user() message
   LangChain4j: ContentRetriever → AiServices.builder().contentRetriever(retriever).build()
                RAG injected automatically — no manual context building
   Winner: LangChain4j (RAG wired declaratively via builder)

D: Tool calling:
   Spring AI: @Tool annotation + ChatClient.Builder.defaultTools(bean) + .prompt().call()
   LangChain4j: @Tool annotation + AiServices.builder().tools(bean).build()
   Winner: Tie — both are similar; Spring AI slightly more Spring-idiomatic

OVERALL:
  LangChain4j: cleaner interfaces, simpler memory/RAG, less boilerplate
  Spring AI: better Spring Boot integration, more control over prompt details
  Production choice: depends on team preference; both are production-ready

hints: LangChain4j: declarative (interface annotations)
Spring AI: programmatic (ChatClient methods)
@MemoryId: LangChain4j's killer feature for conversation memory

exercises.title: Complete the LangChain4j AiService interface
exercises.instructions: Fill in the LangChain4j interface declaration.

blanks.hint: per-session memory — unique per user session

hints: @SystemMessage = Spring AI's defaultSystem()
@UserMessage = the user's question parameter
@MemoryId = unique session identifier for automatic memory management

exercises.title: Basic AiService interface
exercises.instructions:
Write a LangChain4j AiService for HDFC Bank:
1. HdfcBankAI interface: answer(@UserMessage String question)
2. @SystemMessage: HDFC Bank assistant persona
3. AiServices.create() demo
4. @SpringBootTest: interface and annotation present
hints: @AiService: Spring Boot auto-configures the implementation bean
@SystemMessage on the method: applies to every call
@UserMessage on the parameter: LangChain4j uses this as the user's input

exercises.title: AiService with conversation memory
exercises.instructions:
Build conversation memory with LangChain4j:
1. RazorpayChatAI interface: chat(@UserMessage message, @MemoryId sessionId)
2. MessageWindowChatMemory: keep last 10 messages
3. ChatMemoryProvider: one memory store per session ID
4. @SpringBootTest: memory provider configured correctly
hints: @MemoryId: unique per user session (UUID, HTTP session ID, or user ID)
maxMessages=10: sliding window — oldest message dropped when limit hit
Different sessions: ChatMemoryProvider creates separate memory per session ID

exercises.title: AiService with @Tool methods
exercises.instructions:
Add @Tool methods to LangChain4j AiService:
1. PaymentToolsService: @Tool getPaymentStatus(paymentId), getRefundETA(paymentId)
2. RazorpayPaymentAI interface with access to tools
3. AiServices.builder().tools(toolsService) wires them together
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): tools defined, builder pattern documented
hints: @Tool(description): the description tells the AI when to call this method
Tools vs Spring AI: same concept, different import (dev.langchain4j.agent.tool.Tool)
AI decides when to call: 'What is PAY-001 status?' → AI calls getPaymentStatus('PAY-001')

exercises.title: LangChain4j RAG with ContentRetriever
exercises.instructions:
Write LangChain4j RAG configuration:
1. InMemoryEmbeddingStore with 3 Razorpay policy TextSegments
2. EmbeddingStoreContentRetriever: topK=3, minScore=0.7
3. RazorpayDocsAI interface: answer with RAG
4. @SpringBootTest: retriever configured, segments stored
hints: EmbeddingStoreContentRetriever: plugs into AiServices.builder().contentRetriever()
maxResults=3: return top 3 relevant segments
minScore=0.7: reject segments with similarity < 70%

exercises.title: LangChain4j streaming response
exercises.instructions:
Write LangChain4j streaming interface:
1. TokenStream return type: `TokenStream streamAnswer(@UserMessage String q)`
2. StreamingController: convert TokenStream to SSE Flux<String>
3. Error handler: onError callback
4. @SpringBootTest: interface has correct return type
hints: TokenStream: LangChain4j's streaming type (vs Flux<String> in Spring AI)
Flux.create(sink -> ...): bridge LangChain4j callbacks to reactive stream
onNext/onComplete/onError: three callbacks for streaming lifecycle

exercises.title: LangChain4j structured output
exercises.instructions:
Get structured output from LangChain4j AiService:
1. TicketRoute record: team, urgency, summary, estimatedResolutionHours
2. RouteTicketAI interface: method returning TicketRoute directly
3. LangChain4j infers JSON schema from the record type
4. @SpringBootTest: method return type is TicketRoute
hints: LangChain4j structured output: declare return type as record → automatic JSON schema
No BeanOutputConverter needed: LangChain4j infers schema from return type reflection
TicketRoute record: each field becomes a JSON field in the AI's output

exercises.title: Complete LangChain4j AiService configuration
exercises.instructions:
Write complete LangChain4j Spring Boot configuration:
1. @Bean ChatLanguageModel: Anthropic Claude (from env var)
2. @Bean ChatMemoryProvider: MessageWindowChatMemory maxMessages=10
3. @Bean ContentRetriever: from InMemoryEmbeddingStore
4. RazorpaySupportAI interface: memory + RAG
5. @SpringBootTest: all beans configured
hints: ChatMemoryProvider as @Bean: LangChain4j injects it via AiServices builder
InMemoryEmbeddingStore @Bean: used by ContentRetriever
Sessions are independent: sessionChatMemory.get('X') != get('Y')

exercises.title: Memory ID design analysis
exercises.instructions:
Analyze these @MemoryId choices for a Razorpay merchant support chatbot. Rate each 1-5 (5=best) and explain the issue:

A: `ai.chat(msg, "default")`  — hardcoded string
B: `ai.chat(msg, merchantId)` — merchant's Razorpay merchant ID
C: `ai.chat(msg, UUID.randomUUID().toString())` — new UUID per message
D: `ai.chat(msg, sessionId)` — HTTP session ID
E: `ai.chat(msg, merchantId + ":" + conversationId)` — composite key
exercises.expected_solution:
A: `ai.chat(msg, "default")` → Rating: 1/5
   CRITICAL BUG: all merchants share the same memory
   Merchant A's conversation visible to Merchant B
   Security/privacy violation — never use a hardcoded MemoryId in production

B: `ai.chat(msg, merchantId)` → Rating: 3/5
   Better: each merchant has isolated memory
   Problem: only ONE conversation per merchant (ever)
   Merchant starts new conversation: still sees old conversation history
   Old history may contain irrelevant context or even security issues
   Use for: single-session tools where you want persistent history

C: `ai.chat(msg, UUID.randomUUID().toString())` → Rating: 1/5
   New UUID per MESSAGE = new memory per message
   AI has no memory at all (worse than no MemoryId)
   Each message is a fresh conversation — can't reference previous messages
   Defeats the entire purpose of @MemoryId

D: `ai.chat(msg, sessionId)` → Rating: 4/5
   HTTP session ID: isolated per browser session
   Memory cleared when session expires (automatic cleanup)
   Problem: if session expires mid-conversation, memory lost
   Good for: web chatbot with HTTP sessions

E: `ai.chat(msg, merchantId + ":" + conversationId)` → Rating: 5/5
   Best: isolated per merchant AND per conversation
   merchantId: security boundary (no cross-merchant memory)
   conversationId: separate memory for each chat session
   conversationId can be UUID generated on first message of a new conversation
   Memory cleared at end of conversation: prevents infinite growth
   This is the production pattern for multi-tenant AI chat

test_cases.expected:
A: `ai.chat(msg, "default")` → Rating: 1/5
   CRITICAL BUG: all merchants share the same memory
   Merchant A's conversation visible to Merchant B
   Security/privacy violation — never use a hardcoded MemoryId in production

B: `ai.chat(msg, merchantId)` → Rating: 3/5
   Better: each merchant has isolated memory
   Problem: only ONE conversation per merchant (ever)
   Merchant starts new conversation: still sees old conversation history
   Old history may contain irrelevant context or even security issues
   Use for: single-session tools where you want persistent history

C: `ai.chat(msg, UUID.randomUUID().toString())` → Rating: 1/5
   New UUID per MESSAGE = new memory per message
   AI has no memory at all (worse than no MemoryId)
   Each message is a fresh conversation — can't reference previous messages
   Defeats the entire purpose of @MemoryId

D: `ai.chat(msg, sessionId)` → Rating: 4/5
   HTTP session ID: isolated per browser session
   Memory cleared when session expires (automatic cleanup)
   Problem: if session expires mid-conversation, memory lost
   Good for: web chatbot with HTTP sessions

E: `ai.chat(msg, merchantId + ":" + conversationId)` → Rating: 5/5
   Best: isolated per merchant AND per conversation
   merchantId: security boundary (no cross-merchant memory)
   conversationId: separate memory for each chat session
   conversationId can be UUID generated on first message of a new conversation
   Memory cleared at end of conversation: prevents infinite growth
   This is the production pattern for multi-tenant AI chat

hints: Hardcoded MemoryId: all users share same memory = security violation
New UUID per message: no memory at all
Composite key: best isolation for multi-tenant systems

exercises.title: Complete Razorpay support AI with LangChain4j
exercises.instructions:
Build complete LangChain4j Razorpay support system:
1. RazorpaySupportAI interface: @SystemMessage, chat(@MemoryId sessionId)
2. PaymentLookupTools: @Tool methods getStatus, getRefundStatus
3. ChatMemoryProvider: per-session memory
4. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): tools work, memory isolated, structured record
hints: @Tool: marks method as AI-callable tool
@MemoryId: unique session for isolated memory
ChatMemoryProvider @Bean: Spring injects into AiServices builder automatically

exercises.title: LangChain4j agent with multi-step reasoning
exercises.instructions:
Build a LangChain4j agent that reasons in multiple steps:
1. ZerodhaTradeAdvisor interface: @SystemMessage with chain-of-thought instructions
2. Tools: getStockPrice, getPortfolioBalance, calculateRisk
3. @UserMessage: trading question that requires multiple tool calls
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): tools functional, advisor interface structured
hints: Chain-of-thought: system prompt instructs AI to call all 3 tools in sequence
Multi-step: AI calls getStockPrice → getPortfolioBalance → calculateRisk → combines into advice
Tools return String: LangChain4j handles type conversion

exercises.title: LangChain4j vs Spring AI comparison implementation
exercises.instructions:
Implement the same feature in BOTH frameworks:
1. Feature: HDFC Bank ticket classification (LOAN/CARD/ACCOUNT/OTHER)
2. Spring AI version: ChatClient + BeanOutputConverter
3. LangChain4j version: AiService interface with typed return
4. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): both produce same result type, both have correct config
hints: Spring AI: manual converter.getFormat() + converter.convert()
LangChain4j: automatic schema from return type — no converter needed
Same record: both frameworks produce TicketClassifResult — different paths

exercises.title: Multi-tenant AI service isolation
exercises.instructions:
Build multi-tenant LangChain4j AI for Razorpay (merchants isolated):
1. TenantIsolatedAI: session key = merchantId + ':' + conversationId
2. Verify: Merchant A cannot see Merchant B's history
3. Conversation expiry: clear memory after 30 minutes
4. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): isolation, expiry detection, composite key format
hints: MRC-001:conv-1 vs MRC-002:conv-1: different keys → different memory
MRC-001:conv-1 vs MRC-001:conv-2: different conversations → different memory
Expiry: remove from map after 30 minutes of inactivity — prevent memory leak

exercises.title: Complete M26 T4 LangChain4j synthesis
exercises.instructions:
Build complete M26 T4 synthesis:
1. AiService interface: @SystemMessage, @UserMessage, @MemoryId
2. @Tool methods: 2 payment lookup tools
3. ChatMemoryProvider: per-session
4. TenantKeyBuilder: merchantId:conversationId composite key
5. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): interface annotations, tools, memory isolation, key format
6. All 4 pass
hints: @AiService + @SystemMessage: reflection verifiable in tests
Tools: test independently — don't need real AI call
Key format: 'MRC-001:conv-1' verified by isValid()
